query: Premortem this nightly batch ETL job that dedupes a customer table. It is plain SQL plus a Python wrapper on a cron schedule. There is no model, no online serving, and no agent.
expected_behavior: Engages the premortem but explicitly states which perspectives have little anchored to attack on a model-less batch job (for example ML Engineer, Red Teamer, Governance) and why, rather than forcing all six full lenses
Concentrates the applicable perspectives on the real artifact (Data Scientist on dedupe correctness and idempotency, MLOps/SRE on rollback, scheduling, and partial-failure recovery)
Does NOT fabricate findings about nonexistent components such as a model, online serving, or an agent in order to fill out all six perspectives
